---
title: Microsoft Intune Endpoint Compliance Adapter — Adapter Technical Specification
doc-type: ats
adapter-id: intune
adapter-class: conformance
mission-class: telemetry
status: reserved
phase: phase-planning
gcc-boundary: gcc-moderate
canon-source: src/uiao/canon/adapter-registry.yaml
derived-from: uiao/canon (sync_canon.py)
generated: '2026-04-16'
aspirational: true
---

# Microsoft Intune Endpoint Compliance Adapter — Adapter Technical Specification

::: {.callout-note}
## Canon-derived document

**Status:** `reserved` · **Class:** `conformance` · **Mission:** `telemetry` · **Phase:** `phase-planning`

**Canon source:** `canon/adapter-registry.yaml` (propagated by `uiao/tools/sync_canon.py`).

The YAML frontmatter and this banner are regenerated from canon on every sync. **Do not hand-edit.** Author new material only below the `## Overview` heading.
:::

## Overview

The Microsoft Intune Endpoint Compliance Adapter is the **read-only telemetry edge** on the M365 device plane. As a conformance-class telemetry adapter, it observes endpoint compliance state via the Microsoft Graph Intune surfaces (`/deviceManagement/managedDevices`) and Defender for Endpoint, producing object-keyed claims and KSI-anchored evidence with compliant / non-compliant counts.

The adapter is the natural complement to two change-making siblings:

- **[`m365`](../m365/m365.html)** — the modernization adapter that manages tenant configuration. m365 authors what *should* be true; intune observes what *is* true on the device fleet.
- **[`entra-policy-targeting`](../entra-policy-targeting/entra-policy-targeting.html)** — binds existing Intune configuration profiles to OrgTree dynamic groups. intune observes whether the resulting compliance evaluations land where canon expects them to.

Two registry entries cover Intune for symmetric reasons. This entry (in `adapter-registry.yaml`) is the **conformance / telemetry** side — read-only state observation. A parallel **modernization / integration** reserved slot exists in `modernization-registry.yaml` to cover the change-making authoring surface (configuration profiles, compliance policies, security baselines, Autopilot profiles, app deployment policies) per ADR-049; that allocation will activate when the in-flight Python implementation at `src/uiao/adapters/intune_adapter.py` is reconciled to the per-adapter ADR contract.

**Implementation:** `src/uiao/adapters/intune_adapter.py`. Conformance: 30/30 PASS.

## Scope

Target surfaces / subsystems: `device-compliance`, `endpoint-protection`, `configuration-profiles`, `update-compliance`

**Reads:** Microsoft Graph API (`/deviceManagement/managedDevices`) for device compliance state, OS version, management agent, last sync time, and endpoint protection status. Read-only client-credential OAuth flow with the documented least-privilege scopes.

**Emits:** `ClaimSet` with one claim per managed device (compliance_state, os_version, device_name, management agent, last sync), `EvidenceObject` with compliant / non-compliant / total counts, plus the `intune-compliance-findings.json` and `endpoint-drift-report.json` artifacts consumed by the continuous-monitoring pack.

**Does NOT:** modify device configuration or compliance policies, access device content, enroll or unenroll devices, or apply remediation actions. This is the *observation* surface for the Intune fleet; the *authoring* surface lives in the modernization-side reserved slot (ADR-049) and `entra-policy-targeting`.

## Controls

NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 controls this adapter supports: `CM-8`, `SI-2`, `CA-7`, `SC-7`

| Control | Role | Adapter capability |
|---|---|---|
| **CM-8** Information System Component Inventory | Primary | Managed-device enumeration with OS version, management agent, and last-sync timestamp; the canonical device-side inventory for compliance reporting. |
| **SI-2** Flaw Remediation | Supporting | Update-compliance tracking feeds patch/update remediation monitoring; out-of-date devices surface as monitoring evidence. |
| **CA-7** Continuous Monitoring | Primary | Scheduled compliance collection provides continuous endpoint posture evidence with the freshness-window semantics defined in canon. |
| **SC-7** Boundary Protection | Supporting | Device-compliance state feeds zero-trust boundary decisions (Conditional Access compliance grant controls, Intune compliance-based access). |

## Operational profile

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Runtime | `python-3.12` |
| Runtime pin | `TBD` |
| Runner class | `github-hosted` |
| Tenancy | `per-customer` |
| Evidence class | `interval` |
| Retention | `3` year(s) |

## Canon invariants

- `gcc-boundary: gcc-moderate`
- `ssot-mutation: never`
- `certificate-anchored: true`
- `object-identity-only: true`

## Notes from canon

Tier 4 conformance adapter. Endpoint compliance telemetry via Microsoft Graph API (Intune device compliance + Defender for Endpoint). Natural complement to the [`m365`](../m365/m365.html) modernization adapter and the [`entra-policy-targeting`](../entra-policy-targeting/entra-policy-targeting.html) OrgPath actuator. Read-only — observes device state, never mutates configuration.

Advisory mapping (per ADR-043 D4 / UIAO_132 §5, pending FedRAMP RFC-0026 ratification): this adapter satisfies requirement RV5-CA07-VLN (endpoint-compliance telemetry) on the traditional FedRAMP authorization pathway, with the modernized pathway gated on publication of the VDR Balance Improvement Release.

## References

- UIAO-CANON-003
- ADR-043 — FedRAMP RFC-0026 pathway alignment
- ADR-049 — Modernization-side intune reserved slot allocation
- UIAO_132 — Continuous Monitoring Pack
- https://github.com/FedRAMP/community/discussions/130

---

*Generated by `uiao/tools/sync_canon.py`. See `uiao/ARCHITECTURE.md` §4 for the cross-repo sync contract. See `uiao-docs/_quarto.yml` for rendering configuration.*
